_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-28 02:54:14Z)**_

After reviewing the diff and the surrounding file:

**Analysis:**
- `docs/vercel_mockup/src/MOCKUP.jsx` is a static React UI mockup for Vercel preview/design purposes (18,121 lines, all in-file mock data).
- Every change is one of:
  1. Adding `t = T.en` parameter (default to in-file constant dictionary).
  2. Replacing a hardcoded English string literal with a `{t.someKey}` JSX text interpolation.
  3. Adding a new vehicle reservation UI block (checkbox + button picker bound to local `useState` — no sink, no I/O).
- `T` is a const object defined in the same file (line 16195). It is not sourced from URL params, fetch, props passed from outside, or any attacker-influenced channel.
- All `{t.X}` usages are React text children, which React auto-escapes — even if `t` were attacker-controlled, there is no HTML/JS sink (no `dangerouslySetInnerHTML`, no `href={...}`, no `src={...}`, no `eval`, no `fetch`/`XMLHttpRequest`/`window.location`, no `URLSearchParams` anywhere in the file — verified by grep).
- The new vehicle-reservation block is pure presentational state (`withVehicle`, `vehicleType`) feeding only into conditional renders of static strings/images. No state crosses a trust boundary.
- No authorization checks, parsers, validators, allowlists, CI configs, or IaC are touched. No backend code is touched.

No security-relevant source→sink path is introduced by this change.
